title: Hosted and Self-Hosted Runners, Isolation, Capacity, and Compatibility · Delivery-System Defense
summary: A release board compares near-miss decisions for hosted and self-hosted runners, isolation, capacity, and compatibility and requires the learner to defend the smallest safe runner isolation and capacity policy. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for runner isolation and capacity policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Pin or record runner OS, architecture, installed software, action runtime, image release, and migration assumptions instead of trusting latest as immutable.
Threat-model persistence, workspace residue, credentials, network reachability, daemon sockets, labels, groups, and repository eligibility for self-hosted runners.
Design single-job ephemeral runner registration, identity, network segmentation, secret delivery, teardown, log retention, and orphan cleanup.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Ci Hosted Runner Image Contract
steps.instruction:
Order the Ci Hosted Runner Image Contract delivery-system evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Ci Hosted Runner Image Contract.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Ci Hosted Runner Image Contract assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Ci Hosted Runner Image Contract, keep Ci Hosted Runner Image Contract connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.text: A quiz public pull request reaches a persistent self-hosted runner with Docker authority and cloud credentials while 48 jobs queue. Bound eligibility, ephemerality, compatibility, capacity, evidence, and destruction. Competency probe: Pin or record runner OS, architecture, installed software, action runtime, image release, and migration assumptions instead of trusting latest as immutable.
